_meta.purpose: Microbiology culture + antimicrobial susceptibility (AST/antibiogram) interpretation aids for the rules engine + LLM: how to read S/I/R, key resistance phenotypes to flag, and specimen-quality rules. NOT a replacement for a validated AST system + local breakpoints (EUCAST/CLSI). Decision support only.
_meta.source: EUCAST/CLSI concepts + standard clinical microbiology. Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS; apply CURRENT local breakpoints.

S.name: Susceptible (standard dosing)
S.meaning: High likelihood of therapeutic success at standard dose.

I.name: Susceptible, increased exposure
I.meaning: Success likely if higher dose / higher exposure at the site.

R.name: Resistant
R.meaning: High likelihood of failure even with increased exposure — avoid.

mrsa.name: MRSA
mrsa.note: Use glycopeptide/alternative; infection-control isolation. See gram_positive_cocci_clusters.

ampc.name: AmpC
ampc.note: Avoid 3rd-gen cephalosporins even if reported S (risk of on-therapy resistance).

cre.name: CRE / carbapenemase
cre.note: MAJOR alert: strict isolation, notify IPC, limited options (novel BL/BLI, colistin).

difficile.name: Clostridioides difficile
difficile.note: Isolate (spore); stop offending antibiotics; oral vancomycin/fidaxomicin.

penicillin_resistant_pneumococcus.name: Penicillin-resistant S. pneumoniae
penicillin_resistant_pneumococcus.note: Meningitis breakpoints differ — use meningitis interpretation for CSF isolates.

sputum.name: Sputum quality (Bartlett/Q score)
sputum.rule: Accept if >25 neutrophils and <10 squamous epithelial cells per LPF
sputum.note: Many squamous cells -> saliva contamination, reject/recollect.

blood_culture.name: Blood culture
blood_culture.note: Single CoNS from one set often contaminant; same organism in >=2 sets or with sepsis -> significant. Time-to-positivity aids interpretation.

urine.name: Urine culture
urine.rule: >=10^5 CFU/mL single organism (or lower with symptoms/catheter) = significant
urine.note: Mixed growth / many epithelials -> contamination, recollect (MSU).
